description: V5 of continuous Q_f facts. Targeting borderline failures with sharper phrasing.

facts.context: The Q_f = Σᵢ<ⱼ ΓᵢΓⱼ f(rᵢⱼ) conservation law for point vortices extends to continuous 2D Euler vorticity ω(x). The continuous analog Q_f[ω] is:
facts.truth: a double integral ∫∫ ω(x)ω(y) f(|x-y|) dx dy that is approximately conserved
distractors: a double integral ∫∫ ω(x)ω(y) f(|x-y|) dx dy that is exactly conserved always
a single-point integral ∫ ω(x)² f(|x|) dx over the vorticity squared
a gradient-weighted integral ∫∫ |∇ω(x)| |∇ω(y)| f(|x-y|) dx dy

facts.context: For 2D Euler flow, the Q_f integral with f(r) = -ln(r) equals:
facts.truth: the kinetic energy of the flow
distractors: the total enstrophy of the flow
the angular momentum of the flow
the total helicity of the flow

facts.context: For 2D inviscid Euler vorticity ω, the quantity Q_{e^(-r)}[ω] = ∫∫ ω(x)ω(y) e^{-|x-y|} dx dy is:
facts.truth: approximately conserved with excellent precision in numerical simulations
distractors: exactly conserved as a Casimir invariant of the 2D Euler equations
not conserved at all and grows exponentially with time in simulations
conserved only for axisymmetric vorticity fields, not for general flows

facts.context: For 2D Euler flow, Q_{√r}[ω] = ∫∫ ω(x)ω(y) √|x-y| dx dy is:
facts.truth: approximately conserved like other Q_f quantities with smooth kernels
distractors: exactly conserved as a consequence of Noether's theorem for 2D Euler
not conserved because √r is not smooth at the origin and diverges
conserved only in special cases where vorticity has compact support

facts.context: Comparing Q_f = Σ ΓᵢΓⱼ f(rᵢⱼ) with unweighted Σ f(rᵢⱼ), the circulation weighting:
facts.truth: yields much better conservation in numerical experiments
distractors: makes no difference to conservation quality whatsoever
actually degrades conservation by adding spurious terms
only matters for systems with zero net circulation

facts.context: For 3D Euler vorticity, vortex stretching affects Q_f conservation. Under stretching by factor s, Q_f scales as:
facts.truth: quadratic in s because vorticity magnitude scales linearly with stretching
distractors: constant in s because stretching exactly preserves the Q_f integral
linear in s because Q_f tracks vorticity magnitude directly
inversely quadratic in s due to volume conservation constraints

facts.context: In 3D Euler, the kinetic energy equals Q_{1/r}. Under vortex stretching, kinetic energy is:
facts.truth: exactly conserved in inviscid flow regardless of stretching
distractors: increasing because stretching amplifies vortex motion
decreasing because stretching dissipates energy
undefined because Q_{1/r} does not apply to 3D flow

facts.context: The ratio R_f = Q_{e^(-r)} / Q_{1/r} for 3D vortex dynamics is:
facts.truth: more stable than either Q_f alone due to cancellation of stretching effects
distractors: less stable than either Q_f alone due to ratio amplification effects
identically constant because ratios of conserved quantities are conserved
undefined whenever the denominator Q_{1/r} changes during evolution

facts.context: The Q_f conservation mechanism in vortex dynamics works because when ω(x)ω(y) is large:
facts.truth: both points have similar velocities so advective contributions cancel
distractors: both points have zero vorticity making the integrand vanish locally
the kernel f diverges creating a singular contribution that integrates to zero
the flow is locally irrotational and material derivatives vanish identically

facts.context: The 2D Euler enstrophy Z = ∫ ω² dx is:
distractors: approximately conserved with slow decay in all simulations
not conserved unless the vorticity has zero mean value
only conserved for flows with simply-connected domains

facts.context: For Navier-Stokes flow with viscosity ν, the decay rate of Q_f quantities is:
facts.truth: proportional to ν, recovering conservation as viscosity vanishes
distractors: proportional to ν², making decay negligible at small viscosity
independent of ν, violating continuity with inviscid equations
proportional to √ν with anomalous scaling at high Reynolds

facts.context: If Q_f bounds could control ||ω||_∞ growth in 3D, this would imply:
facts.truth: regularity of Navier-Stokes solutions with no finite-time blowup
distractors: nothing about regularity because Q_f bounds are purely kinematic constraints
guaranteed finite-time blowup because Q_f growth implies enstrophy divergence
only 2D regularity because 3D stretching invalidates Q_f bounds entirely
